title: Potamogeton × cooperi
intro: Potamogeton × cooperi, also known as Cooper's pondweed, is a hybrid aquatic plant species belonging to the family Potamogetonaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and infection, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Potamogeton × cooperi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
